game.title: GE Vernova Strategy Lab
game.subtitle: Portfolio Allocation Simulation
game.description: You are a GE Vernova executive team. Allocate your portfolio across three business segments to maximize cumulative revenue over four market rounds.

power.name: Power
power.description: Gas turbines, nuclear services, hydro — stable demand and strong backlog visibility

wind.name: Wind
wind.description: Onshore and offshore wind — higher growth potential with execution and supply-chain risk

electrification.name: Electrification
electrification.description: Grid modernization, power conversion, high-voltage equipment — structural growth from grid investment

aggressive.name: Aggressive
aggressive.description: Chase the upside — amplifies gains in favorable conditions, exposes you to larger losses

balanced.name: Balanced
balanced.description: Neutral risk/reward — consistent performance regardless of market direction

resilient.name: Resilient
resilient.description: Protect your downside — limits losses in shocks, but caps upside in rallies

events.name: AI Data Center Demand Surge
events.headline: Hyperscalers accelerate data center buildout
events.description: AI compute demand is outpacing grid capacity. Hyperscalers are signing record power purchase agreements, driving urgent demand for gas turbines and grid infrastructure. Capital is flowing into reliable baseload and electrification.

segmentNotes.power: +25% — AI data centers require reliable, always-on baseload power
segmentNotes.wind: Neutral — intermittency doesn't meet hyperscaler uptime requirements
segmentNotes.electrification: +10% — Grid interconnection and conversion equipment in high demand

events.debrief: Teams that over-indexed Power and Electrification captured the AI infrastructure tailwind. Wind allocations were punished for unreliability.

events.name: Offshore Wind Execution Delay
events.headline: Supply chain bottlenecks stall offshore wind
events.description: Permitting delays, cable shortage, and installation vessel scarcity are forcing offshore wind project deferrals across Europe and North America. Investors rotate back to conventional generation as wind backlogs stretch 18 months.

segmentNotes.power: +10% — Gas generation reconfirmed as the reliable bridge fuel
segmentNotes.wind: -25% — Execution failures and deferrals compress margins significantly
segmentNotes.electrification: Neutral — Grid investment continues independent of wind volatility

events.debrief: Resilient strategies protected teams over-exposed to wind. Power rewarded those who maintained allocation.

events.name: Grid Modernization Policy Push
events.headline: Infrastructure bill accelerates grid hardening
events.description: A landmark federal infrastructure package fast-tracks $200B in grid modernization. Demand for high-voltage equipment, power conversion systems, and grid-scale storage surges. Wind projects in the backlog receive accelerated permitting.

segmentNotes.power: Neutral — Steady demand but capital flows toward modernization
segmentNotes.electrification: +30% — Grid hardening is the core beneficiary of the infrastructure bill

events.debrief: Teams that pivoted to Electrification after round 2 are vindicated. The grid modernization thesis plays out.

events.name: Global Macro Shock
events.headline: Credit crunch triggers capital spending freeze
events.description: A sudden geopolitical escalation triggers a global credit crunch. Utility customers defer capital commitments across all segments. Project cancellations spike and order intake falls sharply. Every segment is hit — but not equally.

segmentNotes.power: -10% — Gas backlog provides partial buffer; deferrals still hurt
segmentNotes.wind: -15% — Development-stage projects are most exposed to financing freeze
segmentNotes.electrification: -5% — Government-backed grid work provides strongest resilience

events.debrief: This round rewards defensive positioning. Electrification held up best due to policy backstop. Resilient strategies outperform.
